decision: Fixed onSave in SettingsPage.vue to snapshot settingsStore.error before the await and treat the save as a NEW failure only when errorAfter is non-null AND differs from the pre-await snapshot, so a stale error from a prior failed save is not misread as a failure on the next successful save.
alternative_not_taken: Touch SettingsStore.persist() to reset error.value to null on success — this would have been cleaner but the store is Out of scope (Phase 2 brief §Out of scope: 'The services and stores … No new store field, no error-contract change'). The brief's prescribed fix confined the correction to onSave.
rationale: SettingsStore.persist() only sets error.value on failure and never resets it on success (unlike loadSettings/clearSettings which do). The snapshot approach lets the view distinguish a NEW failure (errorAfter changed) from a stale one (errorAfter === errorBefore) without touching the store. This is the honest minimum within the view layer.
